hooks.prompt:
TASK COMPLETION REVIEW — You MUST evaluate whether the just-finished task contains reusable knowledge worth saving.

Automatically review if ANY of the following are true (do NOT skip):
- 2 or more tool calls were used (Read/Edit/Bash/Grep/Write/etc.)
- A command failed and had to be retried or fixed
- The user corrected you or stated a preference ('I prefer...', 'Always do...', 'Never do...')
- You discovered a gotcha, workflow, or best practice specific to this codebase
- You explained a non-trivial procedure or sequence of steps

If ANY apply, read .claude/skills/ and ~/.claude/skills/ to check for existing skills before creating. Then either:
1. Create/update a skill (.claude/skills/ for repo-specific, ~/.claude/skills/ for cross-project)
2. Append to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for behavioral/architectural insights

Only reply 'Nothing to save.' if the task was truly trivial (single tool call, no errors, no patterns, no corrections).
